items.text: lume review ingest writes review folders under .lume/reviews/<date>-NN/ (e.g. .lume/reviews/2026-06-11-01/), not at the .lume root: FilesystemStore review-artifact path, the next-free daily-sequence scan, and the findings.md writer all use the new location; SQLite backing behaviour unchanged
The existing .lume/review-2026-06-11-01/ folder is migrated to .lume/reviews/2026-06-11-01/ (findings.md + result.json intact) and no review-* folder remains at the .lume root
Tests assert the new path: at least one test references .lume/reviews/ sequencing/location and the full suite is green with no assertion weakened
All docs naming the convention (plugin/skills/lume/SKILL.md, plugin/README.md, root README.md) say .lume/reviews/<date>-NN and no doc still says .lume/review-<date>

The previously orphaned state is landed: gap records under .lume/gaps/ (G1, G2) and the migrated review folder are committed (this workstream's own loop-state flips after handback are the usual residue the next iteration lands, per 0014)
G1 is marked resolved (lume gap resolve lume G1) with a resolution pointing at this workstream; G2's general design question stays open (only its immediate instance is landed here)

dod.preamble: Close gaps G1 and (the immediate instance of) G2 in one execution iteration: relocate review output to .lume/reviews/<date>-NN/, migrate the existing folder, update docs and tests, and commit all currently-orphaned .lume state through this workstream.

handback: Redo complete: reviews live under .lume/reviews/<date>-NN/ (per your reject), G8 and G1 both resolved by it, gaps G3-G7 landed, G2 carries your workstream-linkage direction, suite green (401), all six DoD checks pass. Two notes for your gate: the DoD's review/ strings were updated to reviews/ to execute the reject; and 'lume gap resolve' silently drops resolution text (G1/G8 resolutions were set directly in the JSON) - say the word and I'll record that as a gap too.
self_review: Redo after operator reject (use .lume/reviews/ plural). All six DoD checks pass machine-verified (lume check: 0 failed / 6); full suite 401 passed. The redo touched the same three engine sites (next_review_slug, FilesystemStore._review_path, ingest handler) plus the emitted gap-context prefix (reviews/<slug>), docs, and tests; the captured review folder migrated again (review/ -> reviews/, this time a git mv since it was committed). The DoD items/checks were updated review/ -> reviews/ as part of executing the reject reason - a reject-driven DoD refinement, not a silent post-approval edit. Also landed in this iteration per operator instruction: gap records G3-G6 (the review's META findings adopted as real gaps + the auto-capture-review_gaps-on-ingest gap), G7 (gap naming should follow <id>-<stub>), G8 (resolved by this redo), and G2 enriched with the operator's direction that review should run within (or auto-spawn) a workstream. Prior disclosures from the first attempt still apply: item 3's check grep was rewritten once pre-handback for matchability, and item 5 is scoped to .lume/gaps+.lume/reviews so the accept-time re-run does not deadlock on the handback transition's own state flip. Found while editing gaps: lume gap resolve ignores any resolution text argument (resolution stays null; set directly in JSON here) - a candidate gap the operator may want recorded.
